content: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support (Add Support for Editing Multiple Files). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py.

agent1: I've already made my edits (not adding process_priority). Summary: I did NOT add a 'filenames' param to edit()/Editor.__init__. Instead: (1) in _termui_impl.py renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), which now joins the given filenames into a single quoted-args subprocess command string (editor "f1" "f2" ...). The internal edit()'s call site now does self.edit_files((name,)). (2) in termui.py, edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None' - if it's a str it's wrapped in a 1-tuple, else tuple()'d, then passed to ed.edit_files(filenames). Added two @t.overload signatures for edit(). Since edit_file is renamed to edit_files with a new signature (iterable, not single str), please base your process_priority changes on edit_files(filenames, ...) taking the iterable, and add process_priority as a new trailing param on both Editor.__init__ and edit_files/edit(). Go ahead and layer on top - my changes are already final in my patch.
